summary:
Claim: Case-control studies are best for rapidly assessing the strength of association between smoking and lung cancer.  
Evidence: - They leverage retrospective exposure data, allowing collection within weeks or months after diagnosis. - They avoid long follow-up times required by cohort studies, reducing duration from years to a few months.  
Answer: B
